articles.title: TechCrunch Mobility: A robotaxi ultimatum
articles.summary: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

articles.title: Reed Jobs would rather talk about curing cancer than his last name
articles.summary: When we last sat down with Jobs at TechCrunch Disrupt nearly three years ago, his firm Yosemite was brand new and biotech was still reeling from its post-pandemic crash. Now, the venture outfit has a team of 17; a cluster of blockbuster drugs are all losing patent protection in roughly the same window, creating all kinds of new opportunities; and AI has gone from a curiosity to, in Jobs's words, a huge part of what Yosemite does. "I didn't expect Yosemite to be moving this fast," he said.

articles.title: This slushie machine was a lifesaver during NYC’s heat wave
articles.summary: Ninja's latest slushie machine builds on the popularity of the original Slushi, but with a big upgrade.

articles.title: Smart glasses without a camera? Even Realities bets productivity beats recording everyone
articles.summary: The glasses are targeted at people who might be constantly in meetings, giving presentations, and traveling to countries where different languages are spoken.

articles.title: OpenAI bets on families as ChatGPT goes deeper into households
articles.summary: ChatGPT is hiring a dedicated product manager to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults, according to a job posting.

articles.title: US cybersecurity agency CISA had to build its incident playbook during the incident, agency reveals
articles.summary: Independent cybersecurity journalist Brian Krebs reported in May that a security researcher with cyber firm GitGuardian alerted him to reams of exposed passwords stored in a publicly accessible GitHub repository, which an employee of a CISA contractor had uploaded.

articles.title: Phia accused of ‘cookie stuffing,’ taking affiliate credit on purchases it didn’t earn
articles.summary: Phia, the shopping startup founded by Bill Gates’ daughter, Phoebe, and her friend Sophia Kianni is under fire for a practice known as “cookie stuffing,” which helped the product receive commissions and credit for sales it did not actually generate, per a Bloomberg investigation.

articles.title: Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
articles.summary: "Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give people control over whether their public content could be referenced in this way," the company said in a blog post. "We've heard the feedback that this feature missed the mark, so it's no longer available."

articles.title: Bluesky’s interim CEO, Toni Schneider, drops the ‘interim’
articles.summary: Schneider, who formerly served as the CEO of Automattic and is a partner at True Ventures, says he is "all in" on the unconventional social media platform.

articles.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
articles.summary: Apple alleges the misconduct was directed by OpenAI's senior leadership, including a longtime former employee.

articles.title: Filing: College app Fizz accuses VC of sharing confidential startup information with rival Sidechat
articles.summary: Fizz has expanded its lawsuit against rival Sidechat, alleging that a Maveron VC shared its confidential information obtained during a fundraising meeting with the competing startup.

articles.title: SK Hynix raises $26.5B in the biggest foreign IPO in US history, is urged to build new US fabs
articles.summary: The AI chip boom just produced its biggest Wall Street moment yet. Now SK Hynix and Samsung are being asked to build U.S. factories.

articles.title: A new app, HyperTexting, turns the open web into a scrollable social media-like feed
articles.summary: HyperTexting's new app aims to make the open web feel more like social media by turning websites, blogs, newsletters, and podcasts into a scrollable feed, while also making it easier to post to your own website.

articles.title: China is catching up to Elon Musk’s reusable rockets
articles.summary: China's state-owned space company recovered its first orbital rocket booster after launch.

articles.title: Disney+ is considering a free streaming tier, report says
articles.summary: The launch of free content would allow Disney+ to better compete with free services like YouTube and Tubi, which are capturing a growing share of consumers’ viewing time.

articles.title: Dumb Co dared me to trade my iPhone for a hacked flip phone
articles.summary: Dumb Co sells flip phones that sync to your smartphone, bridging the infinite connectivity of the iPhone and the unrealistic limitations of an early 2000s relic.

articles.title: Oratomic raises $300M to build a viable quantum computer that needs only 20K qubits
articles.summary: The massive round was co-led by ARCH Venture Partners, Spark Capital, and Khosla Ventures.

articles.title: Netflix could be planning ‘always-on’ live TV channels
articles.summary: Amid signs of slowing engagement,  Netflix is reportedly considering launching "always-on" live channels, giving subscribers something to tune into 24/7.

articles.title: EU threatens Meta with fines over addictive features on Facebook and Instagram
articles.summary: The tech giant is in breach of the Digital Services Act by focusing on features like infinite scroll, autoplay, push notifications, and the highly personalized recommendation algorithms, the European Commission said.

articles.title: Florida ransomware negotiator convicted for helping ransomware gang extort US companies
articles.summary: A third ransomware negotiator has been jailed for helping a notorious ransomware group extort American victim companies into paying the hackers.
